headline: How New Marine and Aviation Updates At Garmin (GRMN) Have Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In early July 2026, Garmin introduced the LiveScope 2 Series of live-scanning sonar transducers and rolled out major Garmin Pilot app enhancements for Apple devices, including a redesigned Flights page and expanded SmartCharts coverage into Canada and Mexico. Together, these marine hardware and aviation software updates highlight Garmin’s push to deepen its ecosystem in two of its highest-value specialty markets. We’ll now examine how LiveScope 2’s higher-resolution sonar and broader...

headline: Garmin unveils AXIS, a new generation of highly integrated flight displays
summary: Garmin (NYSE: GRMN) today announced AXIS™, an all-new family of flight displays, designed from the ground up to offer a highly integrated and flexible cockpit display solution. AXIS brings Garmin's latest avionics technology to certified piston-powered single and twin1 engine aircraft via an expansive AML STC covering hundreds of models, as well as experimental and LSA aircraft. In an industry-first, AXIS flight displays come in a variety of models that can include a built-in IFR GPS, NAV/COMM r

headline: Garmin (GRMN) Stock Looks About Right At Current Levels
summary: Garmin stock has delivered a strong 145.1% return over the past three years. At around US$248.68 per share, the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate and earnings multiples both point to a price that now looks roughly in line with what the models suggest rather than obviously cheap. Over the past three years, Garmin has returned 145.1% to shareholders, which puts extra focus on whether the current valuation still leaves enough room for future execution risks.
